title: Workshop: Build a Community Reporting Desk
summary: Workshop: Build a Community Reporting Desk retrieves prior HTML skills in an original community reporting desk build scenario and requires source, parsed-structure, keyboard, and content evidence.
objectives: Apply semantic landmarks, semantic heading outline, semantic text meaning, semantic time address quotes, semantic disclosure, semantic content models, semantic native first in unfamiliar content.
Produce or audit a real community reporting desk build artifact without appearance-only reasoning.
Keep earlier document requirements active while verifying native behavior and valid relationships.

starterFiles.html: <!-- Continue the Heat Relief Network artifact here. -->

steps.instruction: Checkpoint 1: Create the page banner in the existing artifact and rerun every accumulated check. Scenario: community reporting desk build.
steps.why: Workshop: Build a Community Reporting Desk keeps meaning testable instead of depending on appearance. This is checkpoint 1 in community reporting desk build.

content.text: Edit the current community reporting desk build artifact, preserve earlier checks, and verify source plus browser behavior.

options.text: Community reporting desk build case 3: strong and em are merely longer spellings of bold and italic, so they can style any text without changing meaning.
Community reporting desk build case 3: select inline elements from the meaning the sentence needs, and leave purely visual treatment to CSS.
Community reporting desk build case 3: use abbr around unfamiliar full words even when no abbreviation exists.
